e2ee/sha512

        sha512HmacHex

        update
      • update(state: var Sha512HmacState; message: openArray[byte])

      • update(state: var Sha512State; message: string)

This module implements a high-level API for SHA-512 hashing, HMAC, and HKDF key derivation using the underlying Monocypher library

The API includes both one-shot functions for hashing and HMAC, as well as streaming interfaces that allow you to update the hash or HMAC state with multiple chunks of data before finalizing the result.

The HKDF functions allow you to derive keys of arbitrary length from a given input key material (IKM) and salt, using the HKDF construction with SHA-512 as the underlying hash function. The API is designed to be easy to use while still providing the flexibility needed for various cryptographic applications.

For lower-level access to the Monocypher functions, see the monocypher module, and for general utilities, see the utils module.

Imports

private/monocypher, private/utils

Types

Sha512Digest = array[Sha512DigestSize, uint8]
Sha512Hmac = array[Sha512DigestSize, uint8]
Sha512HmacState = object
The HMAC state for SHA-512 
Sha512Prk = array[Sha512DigestSize, uint8]
Sha512State = object
The SHA-512 state

Consts

Sha512BlockSize = 128
Sha512DigestSize = 64

Procs

proc finish(state: var Sha512HmacState): Sha512Hmac {....raises: [ValueError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Finalize the HMAC computation and return the resulting HMAC value. After calling this procedure, the state is marked as finalized and cannot be updated further. If the state is already finalized, an exception is raised. 
proc finish(state: var Sha512State): Sha512Digest {....raises: [ValueError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Finalize the SHA-512 computation and return the resulting digest. Once called, the state is marked as finalized and cannot be used for further updates 
proc finishHex(state: var Sha512HmacState): string {....raises: [ValueError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Finalize the HMAC computation and return the resulting HMAC value as a hexadecimal string. After calling this procedure, the state is marked as finalized and cannot be updated further 
proc finishHex(state: var Sha512State): string {....raises: [ValueError], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
proc hkdfExpandSha512(prk, info: openArray[byte]; okmLen: Natural): seq[uint8] {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Expand a pseudorandom key (PRK) using HKDF with SHA-512. Returns an output key (OKM) of length okmLen. 
proc hkdfExpandSha512(prk, info: string; okmLen: Natural): seq[uint8] {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Expand a pseudorandom key (PRK) using HKDF with SHA-512 
proc hkdfExpandSha512[N: static[int]](prk, info: openArray[byte]): array[N,
    uint8]
Expand a pseudorandom key (PRK) using HKDF with SHA-512 
proc hkdfSha512(ikm, salt, info: openArray[byte]; okmLen: Natural): seq[uint8] {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc hkdfSha512(ikm, salt, info: string; okmLen: Natural): seq[uint8] {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc hkdfSha512[N: static[int]](ikm, salt, info: openArray[byte]): array[N,
    uint8]
Derive a fixed-size output key (OKM) of N bytes using HKDF with SHA-512 
proc initSha512(): Sha512State {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Initialize a new SHA-512 state. After initialization, the state is ready to accept message updates 
proc initSha512Hmac(key: openArray[byte]): Sha512HmacState {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Initialize a new HMAC-SHA512 state with the given key. 
proc initSha512Hmac(key: string): Sha512HmacState {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Initialize a new HMAC-SHA512 state with the given key. The key can be of any length; if it is longer than the block size (128 bytes), it will be hashed first. After initialization, the state is ready to accept message updates. 
proc sha512(message: openArray[byte]): Sha512Digest {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Compute the SHA-512 hash of the given message and return it as a digest 
proc sha512(message: string): Sha512Digest {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Compute the SHA-512 hash of the given message and return it as a digest 
proc sha512Hex(message: openArray[byte]): string {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Compute the SHA-512 hash of the given message and return it as a hex string 
proc sha512Hex(message: string): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Compute the SHA-512 hash of the given message and return it as a hex string 
proc sha512Hmac(key, message: openArray[byte]): Sha512Hmac {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Compute the HMAC-SHA512 of the given message using the provided key. The key can be of any length; if it is longer than the block size (128 bytes), it will be hashed first. 
proc sha512Hmac(key, message: string): Sha512Hmac {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Compute the HMAC-SHA512 of the given message using the provided key. The key can be of any length; if it is longer than the block size (128 bytes), it will be hashed first. 
proc sha512HmacHex(key, message: openArray[byte]): string {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Compute the HMAC-SHA512 of the given message using the provided key, and return the result as a hexadecimal string. 
proc sha512HmacHex(key, message: string): string {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Compute the HMAC-SHA512 of the given message using the provided key, and return the result as a hexadecimal string. 
proc update(state: var Sha512HmacState; message: openArray[byte]) {.
    ...raises: [ValueError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Update the HMAC state with a new message chunk. If the state has already been finalized, an exception is raised. 
proc update(state: var Sha512HmacState; message: string) {....raises: [ValueError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Update the HMAC state with a new message chunk. If the state has already been finalized, an exception is raised. 
proc update(state: var Sha512State; message: openArray[byte]) {.
    ...raises: [ValueError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Update the SHA-512 state with a new message chunk. If the state has already been finalized, an exception is raised 
proc update(state: var Sha512State; message: string) {....raises: [ValueError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Update the SHA-512 state with a new message chunk. If the state has already been finalized, an exception is raised
